vlm_note: Board fit clean (empty_baseline residual 0.83px). Pieces read unambiguously: Klevz=RED at v10=[2,3,10] and v23=[6,16,17]; ThePhantom=BLACK at v6=[1,2,8] (+ one of v6/v12/v17-region and v15). BUT the mandatory grant self-check CANNOT pass for Klevz: meta granted_resources Klevz={BRICK:1,ORE:1} (2 cards), and NO vertex on this board produces exactly {BRICK,ORE} (verified by exhaustive topology scan). Klevz's two red settlements are v10=[2,3,10]=(ORE4+BRI5+WHE8)={BRICK,ORE,WHEAT} and v23=[6,16,17]=(SHE10+SHE8+ORE5)={SHEEP,SHEEP,ORE} — neither equals {BRICK,ORE}. The v10 read matches {BRICK,ORE} plus an extra WHEAT, consistent with the grant parser having under-counted Klevz's setup resources by one (dropping the WHE8 card), but that is a hypothesis, not a verified fact. ThePhantom's grant {ORE,WHEAT,WOOD} does match (v6/v12/v17). Fail-closed: declined rather than trust a board/grant pair that does not self-verify. Isolated anomaly — the sibling probe game AabGS_mlY3Y__g1 self-checks cleanly for both players, so this is not a systematic parser failure.
